---
title: "Week 2 - May 22"
author: "Colin Madland"
date: "2026-05-22"
categories: [summer 2026]
image: "arrow-leaf.jpeg"
image-alt: "Bright yellow flower, and Arrow-leaf Balsam-Root with long, slender petals that radiate from the centre. The background contains other flowers, but they are very subdued and out of focus. This flower is very common in the early spring on the slopes of warm, sunny hillsides throughout the Okanagan"
license: CC-BY
---

A few quick updates as we head into the weekend.

Your Multimedia story draft is due on Monday, and [I've published instructions on the course site](https://edtechuvic.ca/edci337/multimedia-story-1/). Just a reminder that I expect this to be a work-in-progress, and not fully refined. I've also linked the the grading rubric that I use. You can find it by clicking the 'Assessment' item under the 'Assignments' menu item. You can also find it on this mirror site at the 'Assessment' link in the left sidebar.

I know you don't have much reason to trust me yet as this is the first assignment you have done for me, but please know that I am interested in your thoughts and process about this and if you make that clear, and connect ideas to the course content, you will be fine. I am not out to find fault in your work.

Also, I have published a 'Learner Posts' page, accessible under the 'Sections' menu item. Feel free to read and interact with other learners' blog posts. The best way to respond to someone is to create a post on your own site that links to the post you are commenting about. That way, the other blog will be notified and your post will show up as a 'Trackback', which is like a comment on their blog.

Finally, I've really enjoyed reading your posts, and I appreciate your work at staying on top of EDCI337 (and other courses as applicable).

As always, I'm cheering you on! Please [reach out if you have any questions or concerns.](https://calendar.proton.me/bookings#ds0BflsDPBRrqaOCr3hDYlrp0Icad1HYmZU-hjUqdiY=)
